factors.name: CPI Release Imminent (Wednesday AM)
factors.summary: US May CPI due Wednesday morning with consensus expecting 4.2% annual rate; any upside surprise would trigger Fed repricing and rate-hike expectations, compressing long-duration tech multiples. Bond traders are already positioning for multiple hikes as early as September per Bloomberg.

factors.name: Japan Inflation Beat — BoJ Hike Signal
factors.summary: Japan May PPI came in at 6.3% y/y (vs 5.5% expected) and 0.9% m/m (vs 0.5% expected); Daiwa sees BoJ June hike as move to avoid 'falling behind curve,' signaling global central bank tightening bias and potential USD strength that pressures NQ components with international revenue.

factors.name: Middle East Escalation — US-Iran Strikes
factors.summary: US launched fresh airstrikes on Iran targets (air defense/radar near Hormuz) after Apache helicopter downed; Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi vowed direct retaliation. Strait of Hormuz closure risk elevated, crude oil spiked intraday but fell $3.10 by close; risk-off sentiment pressures risk assets and extends tech selloff.

factors.name: Tech Sector Momentum Reversal
factors.summary: Jim Cramer noted tech stocks losing key rally qualities; AAPL slid on weak Siri AI reveal, SMCI plunged on $7B equity raise dilution despite AI backlog strength. XLK/QQQ underperforming amid SpaceX IPO jitters and rotation away from mega-cap AI beneficiaries.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising (Inflation Surprise Risk)
factors.summary: Bond traders positioning for faster Fed hikes (September or sooner); nominal yields rising into CPI print Wednesday. Long-duration NDX components (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, TSLA, META) highly sensitive to real rate increases, with 100+ multiple stocks facing compression if inflation surprises hot.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY near 99.90, inverse NQ)
factors.summary: USD/JPY elevated, risk-off flows boosting greenback; strong USD headwind for NQ's international-revenue mega-caps (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL all >40% FX-exposed). Energy Secretary Wright noted Hormuz traffic rising, dampening oil shock fears temporarily but not reversing EM currency pressures.

factors.name: VIX / Risk-Off Mode
factors.summary: Gold tumbled 1.93% below $4,250 on Middle East escalation and higher rate expectations; silver down 3.5% to 11-week low. Risk-off mood and de-risking flows into CPI are headwinds for beta-heavy tech growth names.

factors.name: AI Capex Concerns (SuperMicro Equity Raise)
factors.summary: SMCI announced $7B equity raise despite booming backlog, signaling heavy cash burn and dilution. Market interprets as sign of capex saturation risk; Micron/chip equipment seeing 'supercycle' but visibility into supply constraints is raising valuation concerns.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Angst & Tech Positioning
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO nearly 4x oversubscribed but retail allocation uncertainty causing jitters; investors retreating from hot AI/growth plays ahead of mega-IPO. Tech sector rotation into value/defensive posture.

factors.name: 24h Price Action: -1.08% Intraday Decline
factors.summary: NAS100 down 1.08% over 24h despite +1.31% 4h bounce; suggests weak support and vulnerable bounce. High intraday ranges (Nasdaq 1,279 pt swing yesterday) indicate shallow conviction for any direction ahead of CPI.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in Phase 2 of a downside move driven by confluence of real-rate repricing (Japan inflation beat, Fed hike expectations, CPI print looming Wednesday AM) and Middle East geopolitical risk (US-Iran escalation, Hormuz closure concern, risk-off flows). Long-duration mega-cap tech is the most vulnerable to rising real yields, and AI capex euphoria (SuperMicro dilution, SpaceX IPO jitters) is cracking. The index bounced +1.31% in 4h but remains underwater 1.08% on the day, suggesting the bounce is a bear-trap retest rather than a reversal.
catalysts_ahead: US May CPI release Wednesday 08:30 ET (consensus 4.2% y/y; hot print = rate-hike repricing, bearish NQ multiples)
Fed bank stress test results Wednesday 16:00 ET (June 24)
FOMC decision timing (rate-hike forward guidance if imminent)
Iran retaliation threat (further Hormuz risk escalation)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings (guidance on cloud capex & AI demand sustainability)
Tech sector rotation / QQQ ETF flows into CPI
screening.risk_warning: If CPI comes in COOL (sub-4.0%), real yields fall sharply, and Fed hike bets unwind, NAS100 could rip higher 2–3% as growth multiples expand. Similarly, any Iran-US de-escalation deal or Hormuz reopening would flip risk sentiment bullish and reverse this sell bias. Momentum quant/CTA long positions may also cushion downside if support at 28800 breaks, forcing short covering.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 — CPI print is 16h away; do NOT chase shorts into CPI, as volatility will spike and whipsaws are likely. Better entry: wait for post-CPI reaction to establish direction, then enter on retest of intraday highs (~29200) if bias remains SELL, or short any hot-CPI rip above 29400. Current zone (29050–29150) is chop; avoid.

factors.name: US-Iran Military Escalation
factors.summary: US launched three waves of strikes on Iranian air defense, radar, and Hormuz infrastructure after Apache helicopter downed; Iran's Foreign Minister issued direct threat of retaliation. This introduces acute geopolitical risk premium and Strait of Hormuz disruption risk, historically bearish for tech/growth equities.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Cost Inflation Risk
factors.summary: WTI traded ~$87.40, up on conflict; China PPI hit near 4-year high on Iran war-driven input costs. Rising energy/commodity input costs feed into future CPI prints, raising Fed tightening risk that pressures long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: US CPI Data Imminent (May, due 12:30 GMT today)
factors.summary: Market consensus expects CPI acceleration driven by persistent high oil prices per Iran crisis. Hot inflation surprise would trigger Fed re-pricing and real yield spike, directly compressing NDX valuations. This is THE dominating catalyst in next 6 hours.

factors.name: Real Yields & Long-Duration Tech Sensitivity
factors.summary: Inflation spike expectations + geopolitical risk premium = likely nominal AND real yield rise. Tech mega-caps (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, TSLA) are hyper-sensitive to real rate moves; higher real yields compress forward earnings multiples significantly.

factors.name: BOJ Rate Hike Expectations (June meeting)
factors.summary: ~94% of economists (66/70) expect BOJ to raise policy rate to 1% in June; ~79% expect 1.25% by Q4 2026. BOJ tightening narrows carry trade appeal (key NDX funding source), pressuring risk appetite and growth multiples globally.

factors.name: Asian Risk-Off & Tech Selloff Spreading
factors.summary: South Korea Kospi put/call hedging surged to levels that preceded past selloffs; Asia stocks fell on chip losses and geopolitical jitters. Semiconductor weakness (NVDA, SMH) is a leading indicator for NDX; Lenovo slid ~10% on memory cost inflation.

factors.name: Tech Earnings Cycle Uncertainty
factors.summary: Oracle earnings call looming; SpaceX, Einride IPOs this week. Mixed signals: AI capex narrative (OpenAI-Nvidia Ohio data center, Meta-Reliance India AI hub) remains intact, but earnings must justify stretched valuations post-Trump election rally.

factors.name: BofA Red Flags on Equities
factors.summary: Bloomberg reported BofA warning of 'underlying danger' in US stocks despite rebounds. Reversal in breadth/momentum signals beneath headline gains suggests fragility in the rally.

factors.name: Dollar Steady Near 100.00 DXY
factors.summary: USD remains firm on Middle East geopolitical risk premium and rising real rate expectations. Stronger dollar headwind for large-cap tech international earnings, though not yet accelerating.

factors.name: Previous Sell Signal (2026-06-10 00:12 SELL6/10)
factors.summary: Your prior bias was SELL with no confirmation; escalation in Iran crisis and CPI event risk validate that bearish lean. Intraday bounce (+1.31% in 4h) may be relief rally or rebalance, not structural reversal.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between a structural growth narrative (AI capex, earnings resilience) and acute cyclical headwinds: geopolitical escalation in Middle East, imminent US CPI print likely to show inflation acceleration, BOJ tightening shifting carry/funding costs, and Asian chip weakness spreading. The +1.31% 4h bounce is consistent with short-term relief into overnight volatility, but it masks underlying deterioration in breadth and real yield expectations. CPI release in <18 hours is the fulcrum; a hot print would trigger rapid multiple compression in mega-cap tech. Confidence is moderate-low because (a) CPI is imminent and markets chop into major data events, (b) the 4h move higher may simply be position adjustment rather than trend reversal, and (c) geopolitical risk is hard to price precisely.
catalysts_ahead: US May CPI release (12:30 GMT today) — CRITICAL: expected to show acceleration on oil; surprise could trigger 2-3% NDX sell-off
ECB rate decision (Thursday 2026-06-12 13:15 GMT) — expected hike; impact depends on US CPI outcome
BOJ June meeting (mid-June) — 94% probability of 100bps hike; narrows carry-trade funding
SpaceX IPO (later this week) — major ecosystem test; Elon/tech sentiment bellwether
Oracle earnings (date TBD) — AI capex progress + guidance critical
Iran retaliation window (ongoing) — any strike on US/Gulf assets could spike oil and VIX sharply
screening.risk_warning: If US CPI comes in COOL (below 1.2% m/m or well below expectations), this entire bearish case collapses: real yields would likely fall, Fed rate-hike bets would fade, and tech multiples would re-expand. Also, if geopolitical tensions de-escalate (ceasefire talks gain traction per Trump's recent comments) oil would fall sharply, reducing inflation risk and removing geopolitical premium — bullish for equities. Conversely, a major Iranian counter-strike on Hormuz shipping or US Gulf bases would spike VIX, oil, and real yields simultaneously, invalidating any relief rally and accelerating NDX downside.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (rally underway in 4h, but not extended). Wait for retest of resistance at 29150 or pullback to 29000 before adding bearish exposure. Avoid chasing the 4h bounce; CPI at 12:30 GMT is the real catalyst. If CPI surprises hot, short the rebound; if cool, re-examine bias.

factors.name: US CPI Data Imminent (May Release Today)
factors.summary: Market expects headline CPI to accelerate to ~4.2% YoY (vs 3.8% in April) driven by Iran-war-induced oil/energy spike. FXStreet and RBC Economics both flag hot inflation risk. Markets are pre-positioning for potential Fed rate hike repricing.

factors.name: Hot NFP Shock (Friday Last Week)
factors.summary: 172k nonfarm payrolls vs 85k expected triggered hawkish Fed repricing. Multiple sources note this 'act as a wake-up call for hawkish Fed risks.' Market now pricing higher-for-longer rate expectations into December.

factors.name: Real Yields / Long-Duration Tech Sensitivity
factors.summary: Strong USD and hawkish Fed repricing push real yields higher, compressing Nasdaq multiples. Gold has broken key technical levels and trades below $4,200, signaling elevated real rate expectations that pressure long-duration growth stocks.

factors.name: Tech Earnings / AI Exuberance Cooling
factors.summary: Oracle earnings tonight represent critical test; Reuters/Bloomberg note 'AI stock rally on shaky footing.' SoftBank down 8%+ on OpenAI funding concerns. Semiconductor weakness (Soitec -13% on valuation concerns) signals softening AI capex expectations.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY Support)
factors.summary: DXY holding near 99.90; MUFG notes hawkish CPI print would reinforce 'higher-for-longer' USD strength. Dollar gains pressure Nasdaq's international-revenue-heavy constituents and depress emerging-market tech demand.

factors.name: US-Iran Escalation / Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: US retaliated with 20 strikes against Iran overnight after Apache helicopter downed near Hormuz. Iran signaling tougher negotiation stance. Oil volatility and supply-chain disruption fears weigh on broader equity sentiment, though risk-off is not extreme yet.

factors.name: Tech/Crypto Liquidity Squeeze (SpaceX IPO Oversubscription)
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO 4x oversubscribed; analysts cite 'classic pre-mega-IPO liquidity squeeze' pushing crypto and tech lower. This structural liquidity drain could persist through the IPO completion.

factors.name: Prior Bias History (SELL5/10, SELL6/10)
factors.summary: Previous two reads (5-6 June) were SELL with no confirmation triggered, suggesting caution on conviction. Today's move (+1.31% in 4h) is a modest bounce; alignment with prior bears suggests consolidation within downtrend, not reversal.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility Regime
factors.summary: No specific VIX data in feed; however, Korea Kospi hedging surges suggest elevated tail-risk positioning globally. Options market is pricing uncertainty ahead of CPI.

factors.name: 4h/24h Price Action
factors.summary: NQ +1.31% in 4h but -1.08% in 24h; Nasdaq futures down 0.61% pre-US open. Intraday bounce off lows; not a reversal signal yet. Resistance remains at ~$29,110 per technical analysis.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq faces a critical test today as US May CPI data (expected hot at ~4.2% YoY) looms and Oracle earnings hit post-close. The hot NFP last Friday triggered hawkish Fed repricing, real yields have risen, and long-duration tech multiples are under compression. AI enthusiasm has cooled (SoftBank -8%, Soitec -13%, Oracle earnings uncertainty), while US-Iran escalation and a SpaceX IPO liquidity squeeze add downward structural pressure. The 4h bounce (+1.31%) looks like a retest of support within an ongoing downtrend; resistance at $29,110 remains a key technical ceiling. CPI surprise and FOMC rate repricing are the dominant near-term catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: US May CPI Release (12:30 UTC / 8:30 ET) — Expected headline 4.2% YoY; Core CPI also critical
Oracle (ORCL) Earnings After Close (FOMC-sensitive due to AI/cloud capex signals)
ECB Rate Decision (Thursday 13 June) — Rate hike odds rising on energy inflation
Bank of Canada Rate Decision (Wednesday) — BoC expected to hold; USD/CAD implications
BOJ Monetary Policy Decision (16 June) — BOJ governor Ueda hospitalized; policy likely unchanged but messaging watched
screening.risk_warning: If CPI comes in COOLER than expected (e.g., <4.0% YoY headline, <0.3% MoM core), real yields could compress sharply, triggering a relief rally in long-duration tech and a reversion of the bearish positioning. Conversely, if CPI surprises HOT, Fed rate-hike odds spike, 10Y yields rise further, and NQ faces capitulation selling through $29,000 support. Geopolitical de-escalation (Iran diplomatic reset) could also flip sentiment risk-on, especially if paired with soft CPI.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is PHASE_2 (bounce in progress off lows). Do NOT chase the 4h bounce; wait for a retest of resistance at $29,110 for potential short entry, or hold until CPI print at 8:30 ET (high-impact event — market will reprice within minutes). If CPI hot, look for breakdown below $28,900; if cool, watch for breakout above $29,110 into potential relief rally.

factors.name: CPI Data Release (May 2026)
factors.summary: US headline CPI came in at +4.2% YoY vs +4.2% expected, but month-over-month unrounded was +0.631% (hot). Core CPI m/m +0.2% vs +0.3% expected (softer), YoY +2.9% as expected. Headline inflation at 3-year highs per BLS data, energy-driven (+60% of monthly increase), reaffirms 'higher for longer' Fed stance.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Bond traders maintaining bets on Fed hike by October per Bloomberg 6/10 12:54. Sticky core inflation and headline surprise keep hike-pricing alive into December, pressuring long-duration tech multiples and real yields.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS) / Long-Duration Sensitivity
factors.summary: Inflation surprise + Fed hike bets typically push real yields higher, compressing growth-stock multiples. Nasdaq 100 is heavily weighted to mega-cap long-duration tech (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, TSLA, GOOGL, META) with extreme sensitivity to real-rate moves.

factors.name: US-Iran Escalation & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Trump escalated rhetoric vs Iran (6/10 11:07+), threatened new strikes on infrastructure, raising oil/energy volatility. Earlier US-Iran strikes raised risk-off sentiment; S&P 500 futures down 1.1% (7:45 AM), tech selloff deepening per Bloomberg Brief. Uncertainty delays resolution and props energy inflation.

factors.name: Tech Stock Sector Selloff
factors.summary: SoftBank sinks 8%+ tracking global tech rout (6/10 12:20). Nasdaq-heavy semiconductor names and hyperscalers under pressure. Barclays strategist (MarketWatch) cites exploding investor euphoria + leveraged ETFs as reason to turn cautious on US equities.

factors.name: 4h Price Action: +1.31% rally
factors.summary: NAS100 up 1.31% in 4h but down -1.08% over 24h. Recent 4h bounce may be oversold rebound, not trend reversal. Context: intraday bounce into CPI release, but macro bias remains weak.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Liquidity Drain
factors.summary: Wall Street bracing for mega-IPO (potentially largest in history per Bloomberg). Investors raising cash from tech to fund orders; Tom Lee notes tech dip ahead of IPO will 'reverse afterward' — but near-term pressure is real. Gulf wealth funds bidding billions, pulling capital from equities.

factors.name: Market Positioning & Hedging Costs
factors.summary: Sentiment shifted from FOMO to fear of wipeout. Bloomberg reports costs to hedge $9T S&P 500 rally jump ahead of Fed. Cryptic capitulation signals: 50% of BTC supply underwater, 8M BTC at loss per Decrypt 6/10 11:05. Risk-off flows.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD holding recent gains on sticky inflation expectations. Strong dollar headwind for Nasdaq components with heavy international revenue (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL). However, not dominant factor today vs inflation/Fed repricing.

factors.name: Prior Bias History
factors.summary: SELL5-6/10 bias on 6/10 00:12, 05:17, 09:57 — no confirmation triggered. Today's CPI print and market reaction finally validating the earlier SELL thesis; this is first-cycle confirmation, raising conviction slightly.

key_levels.resistance: 29500–30000 (daily resistance; SpaceX IPO rally target if risk-on reversal)

screening.narrative: US May CPI came in hotter-than-expected on energy, reinforcing 'higher for longer' Fed rate path and pushing bond traders to reprice October/December hike odds higher. Long-duration Nasdaq tech mega-caps (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, TSLA, GOOGL, META) are extremely sensitive to real-yield widening and long-term rate expectations; the inflation surprise + hawkish Fed re-pricing is compressing multiples. Simultaneous US-Iran escalation is raising geopolitical risk premium and energy volatility, adding headwinds. Massive SpaceX IPO liquidity drain is pulling cash from equities. Sentiment has flipped from euphoria to capitulation (BTC 50% underwater). The 4h rally (+1.31%) appears to be an oversold bounce into CPI release, not a trend reversal.
catalysts_ahead: Bank of Canada rate decision (6/10, ~2-3h from now) — BoC likely holds at 2.25%, but forward guidance could shift CAD/risk sentiment
FOMC meeting (likely June 17-18) — next major Fed decision with Ueda-less BoJ meeting on 6/16; watch for hawkish repricing
Next US NFP, PCE release — inflation follow-up data will re-anchor Fed rate expectations
SpaceX IPO pricing & listing (imminent, within days) — liquidity impact and mega-cap rebalancing flows
Trump-Iran headline risk — further escalation could spike oil, push inflation higher, force Fed tightening sooner
Mega-cap earnings season wind-down — any guidance misses on cloud capex or AI demand could trigger NDX reversal lower
screening.risk_warning: If BoC signals dovish hold and market interprets as Fed pause signal, dollar weakens and real yields fall sharply → NAS100 could snap higher. If US-Iran tensions de-escalate or peace deal announced, oil falls, inflation fears ease, growth multiples re-expand. If SpaceX IPO flops or shows weak demand, liquidity flows reverse and tech bid returns. Oversold technical conditions (4h bounce, divergences) could trigger 2-3% relief rally before next leg down.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already 1.3% into bounce (PHASE_2). Avoid chasing short into 4h rally. Wait for retest of 29200–29300 resistance with volume confirmation of weakness, or hold short and target 28500 support if breakdown persists. Intraday traders: use CPI-bounce as short-entry on lower timeframes (5min retest of 29200); target 28800.

factors.name: CPI Hot Surprise (3-yr high)
factors.summary: US inflation hit 3-year high in May 2026 per Bloomberg/MarketWatch. Trump says 'I love the inflation,' but hot CPI typically compresses growth multiples and supports elevated Fed rates, pressuring long-duration NDX.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Hold Signal
factors.summary: JPMorgan and traders expect Fed to hold rates; however, hot CPI sustains expectations for a possible hike by end-2026 per Bloomberg. Real yields likely remain sticky-high, penalizing NDX's mega-cap growth multiples.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield Sticky
factors.summary: Energy prices surging (Iran war cited as driver of inflation), crude up $1.59 to $89.81 per EIA data. Higher energy costs keep nominal yields supported, and long-duration tech sensitive.

factors.name: Semiconductor Weakness Signal
factors.summary: News item: 'semiconductors struggle' with 'mixed signals,' Nvidia under pressure. NVDA is single-largest NDX weight; weakness in semis drags NDX.

factors.name: Divergence: Oracle Surge vs Semis
factors.summary: Oracle surged on AI hype, but this is isolated within mega-cap. Broader semiconductor selloff and earnings revision breadth 'unsustainably high' per Morgan Stanley, signaling near-term rollover risk.

factors.name: European Equities Mostly Lower
factors.summary: DAX -0.88%, France -0.51%, Italy -0.56% per market close data. Global risk-off tone supports NDX weakness continuation.

factors.name: Dollar Strength on Hot CPI
factors.summary: USD uptrend intact despite soft core CPI, supported by strong payrolls and geopolitical tensions per TD Securities. USD strength neutral-to-bullish for NDX carry but typical of risk-off regimes.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran)
factors.summary: Trump escalates Iran rhetoric ('attacking them very hard'). Military tensions typically raise vol and compress growth multiples in cyclical down phases.

factors.name: Prior Bias History: Sustained SELL Signals
factors.summary: June 10 reads show SELL5/10, SELL6/10, SELL5/10 with no confirmation. Today's hot CPI validates prior weakness thesis; move is continuation, not reversal.

factors.name: Price Action: 4h / 24h Down -1.17%
factors.summary: Small drawdown but consistent with macro headwinds. Move is modest in magnitude but directionally aligned with inflation surprise and rate-hold narrative.

screening.narrative: US inflation surprised hot at 3-year high (May 2026), driven by Iran war energy surge. Fed expected to hold rates but real yields remain elevated, pressuring long-duration growth multiples. Semiconductor sector mixed-to-weak (NVDA under pressure), offsetting Oracle's AI-fueled pop. European equities mostly lower, signaling risk-off spillover. Prior SELL bias sustained on macro weakness; this is Phase 2 continuation rather than fresh break.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting (likely next week) — rate decision and forward guidance
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL mega-cap earnings dates (if not yet reported)
PCE inflation data (if not baked into CPI reaction)
NFP jobs report
Treasury yield direction (key for NDX multiple re-pricing)
screening.risk_warning: If CPI is deemed 'high-water mark' and Fed signals cuts later in 2026, real yields compress sharply and growth multiples re-rate higher, invalidating SELL bias. Oracle-led AI enthusiasm could broaden to semis/cloud if hyperscaler capex commentary remains robust. Geopolitical de-escalation (Iran deal closure) would reduce energy price support and ease inflation narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already 1.17% down over 4h; Phase 2 conditions suggest waiting for retest of NDX 29000–29100 resistance or pullback to 28900 before re-shorting. Alternatively, hold conviction on rallies into resistance if macro backdrop remains hawkish (real yields sticky).

factors.name: CPI Miss & Real Yields Higher
factors.summary: US consumer inflation vaulted above 4% (per multiple sources), surprising to the upside amid Iran war energy shock. Higher nominal yields (10Y Treasury auctioned at 4.538% vs prior 4.468%) compress growth multiples directly on long-duration tech.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Shock (Iran-US)
factors.summary: Trump threatens 'attacking Iran very hard,' Israel Defense Minister Katz signals war 'far from over.' Oil rallies to $91.65 intraday (+3.45 to session highs). Risk-off sentiment drives equity liquidation; gold crashes 3%+ but equities weigh down.

factors.name: Tech Sector Heaviness
factors.summary: NAS100 down -1.93% in 24h, -0.24% in 4h; moves showing early extension into day 2. No NVDA, MSFT, AAPL mega-cap earnings catalysts today, but heavy positioning ahead of SpaceX IPO Friday may trap late longs. Gold breaking bear market lows signals peak risk-off.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Mania (Double-Edged)
factors.summary: $75B largest-ever IPO launches Friday with billions in orders from Middle Eastern funds. Euphoria may attract retail dip-buyers short-term, but valuation concerns flagged by NC Treasurer and Sen. Warren; $1.75T valuation with 4.2% float creates liquidity risk post-launch.

factors.name: Treasury Auction Tail / Bid Composition
factors.summary: 10Y auction showed weak domestic participation (12.3% vs 21.1% avg), heavy international buying (78.21% vs 67.6% avg), 0.1bp tail. Suggests some uncertainty in forward rate expectations; foreign buyers stepping in may signal USD strength but also caution on US growth outlook.

factors.name: Sector Rotation: Defensive > Growth
factors.summary: Starbucks cited as 'bright spot' in bleak market; Energy rallying (+2.07% WTI crude). Weakness in biotech (Insmed -52wk low, Graco -52wk low) and semiconductors under pressure. Classic risk-off move away from high-beta tech into staples/energy.

factors.name: Crypto / Risk-On Correlation Breakdown
factors.summary: Bitcoin consolidating near 2026 lows, unable to clear 100/200-day MAs; traders bracing for BoJ rate decision (historically -22.5% BTC response). Crypto weakness typically leads tech weakness by 4-6 hours in major liquidation cycles.

factors.name: Prior Bias History: No Confirmation
factors.summary: Three consecutive SELL reads (5-6/10) with 'no confirmation triggered' suggests technicals have not cleanly confirmed downside. This is PHASE_2 chop—early extension but not a full breakdown yet.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is down -1.93% in 24h and -0.24% in 4h, caught in a PHASE_2 risk-off cycle triggered by hot CPI print (inflation >4%) and escalating Iran-US rhetoric. Treasury yields moved higher (10Y to 4.538%), crushing long-duration growth multiples and suppressing semiconductor / cloud plays. Geopolitical fear coupled with real yield repricing is the dominant story; SpaceX IPO hype on Friday offers potential short-covering/dip-buy relief, but moves have already begun, suggesting late-stage extension risk into the close and overnight. Prior three SELL signals lacked 'confirmation,' indicating chop and potential mean-reversion, not capitulation.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX IPO opens Friday 6/12 (massive retail/sovereign fund interest, volatility spike expected)
BoJ rate decision (expected dovish-hold but could trigger yen move and crypto spillover)
Any additional Trump-Iran escalation statements or Iranian response
Next week: FOMC minutes, major tech earnings (if any unscheduled)
Crude oil inventory data (supportive of higher energy, headwind for growth equities)
screening.risk_warning: If SpaceX IPO launches with euphoric gap-up and retail pours in, NAS100 could see aggressive short-covering rally and mean-reversion buy to 28800+. Conversely, if Iran tensions spike further (direct military strikes announced) or Fed hawkish signals emerge, expect NAS100 to test 28000–27800 support with conviction.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already underway (-1.93% 24h); late to chase shorts. Consider re-entry on retest of 28700–28800 resistance into SpaceX hype, or wait for NAS100 to close below 28200 on high volume for fresh SELL confirmation. Current phase is mean-reversion risk, not breakdown.
